summary: Matched a small set of game-mode and menu functions across gm_1832, mnname, and mnruleplus. The PR replaced placeholder decomp comments with typed C implementations for fn_8018846C, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_802388D4, and mn_80232458, updated their headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures, and added supporting static/global data arrays in mnruleplus. The body states the functions were verified as 100% matching in objdiff and that ninja built cleanly.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8018846C in gm_1832.c as an int-returning no-argument function that reads pl_8004134C(0), indexes lbl_80473700 using Player_GetPlayerCharacter(0) + 2, stores a max value for that character slot, and uses lbl_80473700[67]/[68] as cached or stateful result fields.
Updated gm_1832.h prototype for fn_8018846C from UNK_RET fn_8018846C(UNK_PARAMS) to int fn_8018846C(void).
Implemented mnName_GetPageCount by counting valid name slots over 0x78 entries using IsNameValid((u8) i) and returning a rounded-up count divided by 24.
Implemented mnName_GetColumnCount similarly over 0x78 name slots, returning a rounded-up count divided by 6 and requiring PAD_STACK(16) for matching.

Implemented mnName_802388D4 as an HSD_JObj lookup callback taking HSD_GObj* and u8 index. For indices below 0x18 it walks the child/next chain from the JObj at gobj offset 0x30; indices 0x18, 0x19, and 0x1A return JObjs at gobj offsets 0x24, 0x18, and 0x1C respectively; other indices return the gobj cast as HSD_JObj*.
Updated mnname.h signatures for mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_802388D4 to concrete return and parameter types.
Added mnruleplus data definitions mn_803ED270, mn_803ED294, and mn_803ED2E8 using AnimLoopSettings arrays and a zeroed u8[16][2] table.
Implemented mn_80232458 in mnruleplus.c as a Rules menu helper returning an AnimLoopSettings* selected by option, value, and direction, with options outside 1 through 4 returning NULL.
smart_moves: Converted unknown prototypes to concrete signatures in the headers at the same time as the C implementations, improving call-site type information for future matching work.
Used direct count-and-round-up arithmetic for Name Entry pagination, preserving the observed page size of 24 and column grouping of 6 from the PR description.
Kept the matching-sensitive PAD_STACK(16) in mnName_GetColumnCount, documenting by code shape that the extra stack padding was required for an exact match.

Modeled mnName_802388D4 around actual HSD_GObj/HSD_JObj relationships while still using byte-offset access where the exact gobj payload struct was not established, avoiding premature or unsupported struct invention.
Added explicit AnimLoopSettings data arrays before implementing mn_80232458, making the matched function reference typed data instead of opaque addresses.
Used u8 casts on loop indices and index comparisons where needed, likely preserving the original narrow argument behavior and matching codegen.
decomp_lessons: When a function is matched, update both the source implementation and public header prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the best-supported concrete types.
For menu list pagination, count only valid entries with the existing validity predicate and use explicit rounded-up division when that reflects the original codegen.
Small stack padding macros such as PAD_STACK can be necessary even in simple arithmetic functions; retain them when objdiff requires stack layout alignment.

If a menu object layout is not fully typed, byte-offset field access from HSD_GObj can be an acceptable interim matching tactic, but the returned values should still use meaningful types such as HSD_JObj*.
Array sizes and constants are useful semantic anchors: this PR identifies a 0x78/120-slot name list, pages of 24, and column groups of 6.
Rules/menu helper functions may depend on companion data tables being declared with the right type and dimensions before the function itself can be cleanly expressed.
naming_conventions: Functions still lacking semantic names kept address-based names such as fn_8018846C, mnName_802388D4, and mn_80232458.
Name Entry functions with clear behavior used descriptive CamelCase-style names in the existing module convention: mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount.
Menu module globals retained address-derived names such as mn_803ED270, mn_803ED294, mn_803ED2E8, and mn_804DBE48.
The PR used concrete project types in signatures where known: s32, u8, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, and AnimLoopSettings*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced placeholder '#function' comments with C implementations verified by objdiff according to the PR body.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in mnName_GetColumnCount as an explicit stack-shape matching tactic.
Preserved narrow integer behavior with casts such as IsNameValid((u8) i) and switch/index checks on (u8) index.
Used pointer arithmetic against lbl_80473700 in fn_8018846C, including ptr + Player_GetPlayerCharacter(0) + 2 and fixed indices 67 and 68.
For mnName_802388D4, traversed HSD_JObj sibling links with null checks using child then next pointers, matching a layout callback pattern without fully defining the owner object.

For mn_80232458, encoded exact option filtering and array indexing formulas: direction selects between mn_803ED270 and mn_803ED294, with value == 0 returning mn_803ED270[count] and the reverse direction returning mn_803ED294[count - value].
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body reports all functions verified as 100% matches via objdiff during an overnight run and a clean ninja build.
There was one issue comment counted in the context, but no substantive review-comment excerpt was provided, so no specific reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that rename fn_8018846C or mn_80232458 to semantic names after more game-mode or Rules menu context is understood.
Look for follow-up work that replaces byte-offset access in mnName_802388D4 with a concrete MnName_GObj or menu layout struct.
Check whether mn_803ED270, mn_803ED294, and mn_803ED2E8 later gain semantic names or nonzero initializer documentation.
Search for call sites of mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount to confirm how the 120-slot, 24-per-page, 6-per-column assumptions propagate through Name Entry UI code.
